title: Configure Salesforce Standard Matching Rules for Leads, Contacts, and Accounts
description:
## Context

Salesforce matching rules need to be configured to prevent duplicate records and support data quality across leads, contacts, and accounts.

## Matching Rules

### Lead Matching
Match on: Email OR (First Name + Last Name). Uses fuzzy name matching.

### Contact Matching
Match on: Email OR (First Name + Last Name).

### Account Matching
Match on: Company/Account Name.

## Configuration

Rules should be simple. Geography-based matching conditions were removed for simplicity. Fuzzy name matching is enabled for lead rules.

| Action | Rule Name | Object | Status | Description | Date | Owner |
|--------|-----------|--------|--------|-------------|------|-------|
| Deactivate | Account Name Match | Account | Active | Checks account name match regardless of other attributes | 6/23/2024 | RGood |
| Deactivate | Lead Name Match | Lead | Active | Checks lead name or company name match | 6/23/2024 | RGood |
acceptance_criteria: Lead matching rule matches on email OR first + last name with fuzzy matching
Contact matching rule matches on email OR first + last name
Account matching rule matches on company/account name
Rules are active and functioning in the PBO instance
middleware_changes: Configured Salesforce standard matching rules for Lead (name fuzzy match + email), Contact (name + email), and Account (company name). Removed geography-based secondary conditions for simplicity.
implementation_notes: Matching rules use fuzzy name matching for leads. Geography condition was initially considered but removed to keep rules simple. Rules were configured by Ryan directly.
comment_summary: Ryan added new lead and account matching rules using name fuzzy match and removed the geography condition.
